title: Cobblemon Environment Interactions
description: Turn your Cobblemon partners into useful field companions.
body:
# Cobblemon Environment Interactions

Turn your Cobblemon partners into useful field companions!

Cobblemon Environment Interactions adds Pokemon-powered mining and utility interactions while riding a Cobblemon Pokemon or commanding one nearby companion Pokemon.

`NOTE`: if using pasture blocks, the mod may detect pasture pokemon as companions and therefore ask for only a single pokemon to be out.

`NOTE`: only works with actual Cobblemon Pokemon, not vanilla mobs or other mods' Pokemon.

## Requirements

- Minecraft `1.21.1`
- Java `21`
- Fabric Loader `0.18.4+` and Fabric API `0.116.11+1.21.1`, or NeoForge `21.1.229+`
- Cobblemon `1.7.3+1.21.1`

Optional, but recommended:

- Mod Menu `11.0.3` on Fabric
- Cloth Config API `15.0.140`

For multiplayer, install the mod on both the server and every client.

## Installation

1. Install Fabric Loader for Minecraft `1.21.1`.
2. Install Fabric API and Cobblemon.
3. Put the Cobblemon Environment Interactions jar in your `mods` folder.
4. Start the game.
5. Join a world and press `V` to enable Pokemon interactions.

Pokemon interactions are disabled by default each time you join a world, so normal clicking is safe until you turn them on.

## Controls

| Action | Input |
| --- | --- |
| Enable or disable Pokemon interactions | `V` |
| Mine with a mounted Pokemon | Ride a Pokemon and left-click |
| Use a mounted Pokemon's type utility | Ride a Pokemon and right-click |
| Mine with a companion Pokemon | Sneak-left-click with exactly one owned Pokemon nearby |
| Use a companion Pokemon's type utility | Sneak-right-click with exactly one owned Pokemon nearby |

Companion commands only work when exactly one owned Pokemon is nearby.

## How It Works

Left-click mining breaks blocks using the Pokemon you are riding or commanding. The stronger offensive stat, Attack or Special Attack, controls mining power. Speed lowers the mining cooldown.

Right-click utilities use the Pokemon's type. Some utilities work on the aimed block, while others work on dropped items near the target. Higher stats mean larger areas, more processed items, better rolls, or stronger special outputs.

Held items matter. A Pokemon holding the matching type item gets a `+50` power boost for mining and utilities. Dual-type Pokemon can also use a matching held item to choose which of their types to use.

## Default Tiering And Behavior

Default mining tiers:

| Pokemon mining power | Impact radius | Range |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| Below `50` | 0 | 8 blocks |
| `50-149` | 1 | 11 blocks |
| `150+` | 2 | 15 blocks |

Default utility tiers for most right-click utilities:

| Pokemon utility power | Dropped item limit | Block area radius |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| Below `50` | 8 | 0 |
| `50-149` | 16 | 1 |
| `150-299` | 32 | 2 |
| `300+` | 64 | 3 |

Steel prospecting uses a fixed `3x3` footprint and scales depth instead of radius:

| Steel utility power | Prospecting area |
| --- | ---: |
| Below `50` | `3x3x1` |
| `50-149` | `3x3x2` |
| `150-299` | `3x3x3` |
| `300+` | `3x3x4` |

Default utility cooldowns:

| Speed | Cooldown |
| --- | ---: |
| Below `50` | 10 seconds |
| `50-149` | 5 seconds |
| `150-299` | 2 seconds |
| `300+` | 0 seconds |

Some powerful actions, such as rare crafting, hive harvesting, camp cooking, and large repairs, use a longer cooldown.

## Right-Click Utilities

### Normal

Held item: `Silk Scarf`

- Aim at a lit campfire to create food.
- Better stats unlock better food rolls.

### Fire

Held item: `Charcoal`

- Smelt dropped items.
- Fill empty buckets with lava.
- Light campfires, candles, TNT, and fire positions.
- Fill tiny holes with lava.
- Melt snow and ice.
- Directly evaporate targeted water.

### Water

Held item: `Mystic Water`

- Fill empty buckets and glass bottles.
- Turn concrete powder into concrete.
- Make mud from dirt.
- Place water in tiny holes.
- Extinguish fire and cool targeted lava.
- Fill cauldrons and hydrate farmland.

### Grass

Held item: `Miracle Seed`

- Harvest, grow, and replant crops.
- Turn dirt into grass.
- Turn mud into rooted dirt.
- Grow saplings with moss-themed growth.
- Place vines.
- Make mossy stone variants.

### Electric

Held item: `Magnet`

- Turn sand into redstone.
- Turn torches into redstone torches.
- Toggle copper bulbs on or off.
- Reduce copper oxidation in an area.
- Call lightning on lightning rods.

### Ice

Held item: `Never-Melt Ice`

- Directly freeze targeted water.
- Place snow.
- Fill tiny holes with powder snow.
- Turn water buckets into powder snow buckets.
- Compress snow and ice into packed ice or blue ice.

### Fighting

Held item: `Black Belt`

- Repair dropped gear.
- Smash wood into planks.
- Smash stone into rougher variants.
- Apply stonecutter-style transforms to dropped blocks.

### Poison

Held item: `Poison Barb`

- Brew negative potions.
- Turn arrows into tipped arrows.
- Rot food.
- Create poisonous potatoes and spider eyes.
- Spread mycelium.
- Grow mushrooms or nether wart on mycelium.
- Turn moss into slime balls.

### Ground

Held item: `Soft Sand`

- Till dirt and grass into farmland.
- Fill tiny holes with gravel.
- Turn sand into sandstone.
- Turn red sand into red sandstone.
- Turn mud into packed mud.

### Flying

Held item: `Sharp Beak`

- Shake leaves for sticks, saplings, apples, and cocoa beans.
- Harvest berries and cocoa.
- Clear grass, flowers, vines, cobwebs, snow, and similar light blocks.
- Stir up wild Bug-type encounters.

### Psychic

Held item: `Twisted Spoon`

- Pull nearby dropped items to the player.
- Blink the player to a safe targeted landing spot.
- Blink the mounted Pokemon with the player when mounted.

### Bug

Held item: `Silver Powder`

- Harvest beehives.
- Turn leaves into cobwebs.
- Place cobwebs.
- Turn dropped leaves into cobwebs.
- Compost eligible dropped items into bone meal.

### Rock

Held item: `Hard Stone`

- Carve a tunnel in the direction you are looking.
- Higher stats increase tunnel size and depth.
- Ore blocks are preserved when ore preservation is enabled.

### Ghost

Held item: `Spell Tag`

- Turn grass blocks into sculk.
- Spawn one pumpkin above haunted sculk.
- Turn pumpkins into jack-o-lanterns.
- Turn dropped grass blocks into sculk.
- Condense `9` amethyst shards into `1` echo shard.

### Dragon

Held item: `Dragon Fang`

- Turn glass bottles into dragon's breath.
- Turn obsidian into crying obsidian.
- Turn cobbled stones into end stone.
- Turn leaves into chorus fruit or flowers.
- Turn glass into ender pearls.
- Turn scutes or draconic materials into shulker shells.

### Dark

Held item: `Black Glasses`

- Turn torches, lanterns, campfires, and fire into soul variants.
- Turn glass into tinted glass.
- Turn sand into soul sand.
- Turn dirt and grass into soul soil.
- Turn stone into blackstone.

### Steel

Held item: `Metal Coat`

- Repair damaged anvils.
- Prospect breakable blocks for bonus ore materials.
- Use `3x3x1`, `3x3x2`, `3x3x3`, or `3x3x4` prospecting areas based on Steel utility power.
- Preserve ore blocks when ore preservation is enabled.

### Fairy

Held item: `Fairy Feather`

- Enchant dropped items.
- Grow flowers on grass.
- Brew positive potions.
- Craft enchanted golden apples from apples with gold ingots or from regular golden apples.
- Make `1/2/3/4` enchanted golden apples by utility tier.

## Safety

The mod is designed to be useful without surprising you.

- Interactions stay off until you press `V`.
- Companion commands require exactly one owned Pokemon nearby.
- Mining, Rock tunnels, and Steel prospecting use block-breaking safety rules.
- Many block utilities are surface-aware, so they do not change hidden blocks through a solid cover layer.
- Protection mods can stop mining through Fabric block-break events.

Right-click utilities can still intentionally change blocks or consume dropped items, so treat `V` like a tool mode.

The code release is coming soon.
